title: Books Reviewed
abstract: HAYES, KATHERINE HOWLETT, Slavery Before Race: Europeans, Africans, and Indians at Long Island’s Sylvester Manor Plantation, 1651–1884 by Gregory D. Smithers KRAUTHAMER, BARBARA, Black Slaves, Indian Masters: Slavery, Emancipation, and Citizenship in the Native American South by Gregory D. Smithers LOBEL, CINDY R., Urban Appetites: Food and Culture in Nineteenth-Century New York by J. Michelle Coghlan MOONEY, KATHERINE C., Race Horse Men: How Slavery and Freedom Were Made at the Race Track by Paul E. Johnson OXX, KATIE, The Nativist Movement in America: Religious Conflict in the Nineteenth Century by Luke Ritter BOSTER, DEA H., African American Slavery and Disability: Bodies, Property, and Power in the Antebellum South, 1800–1860 by Rachael L. Pasierowska WEST, EMILY, Family or Freedom: People of Color in the Antebellum South by Trevor Burnard CLANTON, J. CALEB, The Philosophy of Religion of Alexander Campbell by Stephen Kissela JOHNSON, WALTER, River of Dark Dreams: Slavery and Empire in the Cotton Kingdom by Christopher Cumo DORN, T. FELDER, Challenges on the Emmaus Road: Episcopal Bishops Confront Slavery, Civil War, and Emancipation by Terry A. Barnhart ALEY, GINETTE, & ANDERSON, J. L. (EDS), Union Heartland: The Midwestern Home Front during the Civil War by Jennifer L. Weber CARAWARDINE, RICHARD, & SEXTON, JAY (EDS), The Global Lincoln by David Waller RYDELL, ROBERT W., & KROES, ROB, Buffalo Bill in Bologna: The Americanization of the World, 1869–1922 by Flannery Burke KREYLING, MICHAEL, A Late Encounter with the Civil War by Robert Cook American Nineteenth Century History, 2014 Vol. 15, No. 3, 371, http://dx.doi.org/10.1080/14664658.2014.1002172
